channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: OpenAI claims NYT is not telling the full story in its copyright lawsuit
item.description: OpenAI on Monday said that The New York Times (NYT) is not telling the full story about the lawsuit it filed against the Sam Altman-led company and Microsoft on December 27.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Leadership: 3 ostacoli che possono compromettere la fiducia nel CIO
item.description: Una delle maggiori barriere a un’effettiva trasformazione digitale [in inglese] è da ricercarsi nella leadership: quando si spezza il legame di fiducia tra il CIO e il CEO, o tra il CIO e i team del business chiamati ad attuare i cambiamenti, i progetti di digitalizzazione restano incompiuti.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Wi-Fi Alliance starts official certification for Wi-Fi 7 devices
item.description: The Wi-Fi Alliance has begun its official certification program for wireless devices compatible with its latest Wi-Fi 7 standard, also known as 802.11be, marking an important milestone for potential enterprise users.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Study: Bottled water contains thousands of nanoplastics
item.description: Microplastics are polymer fragments that can range from less than 0.2 inch (5 millimeters) down to 1/25,000th of an inch (1 micrometer). Anything smaller is a nanoplastic that must be measured in billionths of a meter.
item.dc:creator: Cnn.Com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: How streaming services copied the cable TV formula—much to its users’ expense
item.description: There’s one thing that television viewers can count on in 2024: higher fees and more commercials. The major streaming services—Amazon, Netflix, Hulu, Disney+, and Max—have all announced rate hikes and new advertising policies.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: The 7 weirdest AI products at CES this year
item.description: If you thought artificial intelligence was inescapable last year, just wait. OpenAI might have skipped this year’s CES, but AI is still everywhere at the show—and, before long, many of the products on display here will be making their way to retail.
item.dc:creator: Chris Morris, Fast Company

item.title: ‘Critical’ fuel leak imperils US company’s moon landing attempt
item.description: The last time the U.S. launched a moon-landing mission was in December 1972. Apollo 17's Gene Cernan and Harrison Schmitt became the 11th and 12th men to walk on the moon, closing out an era that has remained NASA's pinnacle.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Interagency team making it easier for new families to seek out federal benefits
item.description: Interagency teams are also focused on helping the public withstand financial shocks, recover from a disaster, transition from military to civilian life, and navigate retirement.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: GSA finds federal tech accessibility challenges driven by lack of staff, resources
item.description: The General Services Administration found agencies that don't dedicate enough staff to Section 508 efforts often struggle to conform with digital accessibility standards.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Apple’s new Vision Pro ad feels like a sequel to iPhone’s first-ever commercial
item.description: On the same day that Apple announced it would start shipping its new Apple Vision Pro headset in the U.S. on February 2, the brand launched a new ad to hype the mixed-reality piece of hardware.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Beer, Fast Company

item.title: From AI to zero trust, how 2023 will be remembered by federal IT experts
item.description: Federal News Network asked a panel of current and former federal executives for their opinions about 2023 and what federal IT and acquisition storylines stood out over the last 12 months.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: ドイツITZBund、連邦政府のITのクラウド化目指す
item.description: レガシー・アプリケーションの罠に陥ることを避けるため、ドイツ連邦情報技術センター（ITZBund）は、未来志向のクラウド・ランドスケープを実行することを早くから認識していたと、連邦政府のCIO兼副技術部長であるクリスティーネ・セレット氏は言う。ITZBundは連邦行政の中央ITサービス・プロバイダーとして、例えば交通、予算、税金、内部セキュリティ、統合などの分野で幅広い重要なプロセスを運営している。そのため、クラウドファーストは、アジャイルな作業方法、入札や契約締結を評価するインシデント報告ツールであるフェデラルクライアントと並んで、同センターの3つの戦略目標のひとつとなっている。 連邦政府の
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Musk denies using illegal drugs after report about colleagues' concerns
item.description: Billionaire Elon Musk on Sunday adamantly denied using illegal drugs, after the Wall Street Journal published a scathing report detailing purported concerns among some high-level company executives about the SpaceX and Tesla CEO's alleged illegal drug use.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Apple's Vision Pro headset launches next month as company seeks to expand mixed-reality market
item.description: Apple’s high-priced headset for toggling between the real and digital world will be available in its stores beginning Feb. 2, launching the trendsetting company’s push to broaden the appeal of what so far has been a niche technology.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: CES 2024 is upon us. Here's what to expect from this year's annual show of all-things tech
item.description: CES, the Consumer Technology Association’s annual show of all-things tech, is kicking off in Las Vegas this week.
item.dc:creator: Wyatte Grantham Philips, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Five tips for taking better photos with your cell phone
item.description: Want to up your cell phone photography game? Pay attention to the composition and try panos, portraits, lowlight and slow shutter modes.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Gritchen, The Mercury News

item.title: What to know about Boeing 737 Max groundings after Alaska Airlines emergency
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) grounded more than 100 Boeing planes Saturday, after an Alaska Airlines flight suffered an inflight blowout that left a large hole in the side of the aircraft and forced it to make an emergency landing.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Peregrine lunar lander attempt jeopardized by engine problem, company says
item.description: An American aerospace company said Monday that its probe’s lunar landing may not go forward after engine problems at launch that could leave it short of power. Astrobotic Technology, based in Pittsburgh, said its Cape Canaveral, Fla.
item.dc:creator: Nick Robertson, The Hill

item.title: Apple Vision Pro on sale Feb. 2 in US, pre-orders soon
item.description: As CES opens its doors, Apple has started the conversation by announcing its $3,499 Vision Pro headset will on sale in the US on Feb. 2. Pre-orders begin Jan. 19.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Never miss an expiration date again with this hidden iPhone shortcut
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson shows you how to use the Shortcuts app on your iPhone to create and edit expiration reminders for any item that has an expiration date.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boeing jetliner that suffered inflight blowout was restricted because of concern over warning light
item.description: A federal official says the Boeing jetliner that suffered an inflight blowout over Portland, Oregon, was not being used for flights to Hawaii after a warning light that could have indicated a pressurization problem lit up on three different flights.
item.dc:creator: Claire Rush, David Koenig And Becky Bohrer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Are your out-loud conversations fueling ads pushed to your devices?
item.description: In the age of Siri, Alexa and Bixby, your phone is always listening to your out-loud conversations. But can hackers listen, too? Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson shows how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 10 top priorities for CIOs in 2024
item.description: Last year was certainly a wild ride, with the ascendent rise of AI impacting a wide range of IT sectors and capturing the imagination (and fears) of the world at large. IT leaders can look forward to even more surprises, as well as continuing challenges, over the next 12 months.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How do we make money with generative AI?
item.description: Executives who doubt the potential of generative AI are becoming an increasingly rare breed. In a recent survey of Fortune 500 CEOs in collaboration with Deloitte, 75% expected generative AI to improve operational efficiency while over half believed it would increase growth.
item.dc:creator: Kjell Carlsson, Info World

item.title: Getting through the awkward toddler phase of generative AI
item.description: We put up with bad software all the time. Anyone who has raged against their enterprise travel booking machine or tried to decipher the interface to their corporate tool for logging employee feedback knows what I’m talking about.
item.dc:creator: Asay@csoonline.com, Info World

item.title: 10 ways to destroy developer happiness
item.description: Software developers are vital to the success of your enterprise, and as a manager, you need to know how to keep them happy at work. We've already discussed the 9 ways to keep your developer team happy. Now, let's examine the management mistakes that may prompt your best developers to look elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Judges in England and Wales are given cautious approval to use AI in writing legal opinions
item.description: LONDON (AP) — England’s 1,000-year-old legal system — still steeped in traditions that include wearing wigs and robes — has taken a cautious step into the future by giving judges permission to use artificial intelligence to help produce rulings.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judges in England and Wales are given cautious approval to use AI in writing legal opinions
item.description: Judges in England and Wales have been given approval to use artificial intelligence to help writing legal opinions.
item.dc:creator: Brian Melley, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Alaska Airlines again grounds all Boeing 737 Max 9 jetliners as more maintenance may be needed
item.description: PORTLAND, Ore. (AP) — Alaska Airlines again grounded all of its Boeing 737 Max 9 jetliners on Sunday after federal officials indicated further maintenance might be required to assure that another inflight blowout like the one that damaged one of its planes doesn’t happen again.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, DAVID KOENIG and BECKY BOHRER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: These 5 fuzz buster apps can help get you out of a speeding ticket
item.description: Kurt "CyberGuy" Knutsson ranks the top apps to avoid speeding tickets. These apps warn drivers of speed cameras. Some have a monthly cost.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Coal miners in North Dakota unearth a mammoth tusk buried for thousands of years
item.description: Fossil hunters are celebrating the discovery of a mammoth tusk and other bones unearthed at a coal mine in North Dakota.
item.dc:creator: Jack Dura, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FBI warns of 'cyber kidnapping' scam trend
item.description: Groups instruct victims to deceive their friends and family, either to secure funds, act as money mules, or participate in other criminal schemes.
item.dc:creator: Emily Finn, NewsNation

item.title: Samsung to announce by AI-powered phones
item.description: The much-anticipated “all-new mobile experience powered by AI” is the latest instance of artificial intelligence becoming more ingrained in everyday life.
item.dc:creator: Sean Noone, NewsNation

item.title: A group representing TikTok, Meta and X sues Ohio over new law limiting kids' use of social media
item.description: COLUMBUS, Ohio (AP) — A trade group representing TikTok, Snapchat, Meta and other major tech companies sued Ohio on Friday over a pending law that requires children to get parental consent to use social media apps. The law was part of an $86.1 billion state budget bill that Republican Gov.
item.dc:creator: JULIE CARR SMYTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Broadcom’s VMware alterations include layoffs, changes to partner programs
item.description: Broadcom finally closed its acquisition of VMware in November after 18 months of various governments slow-walking the approval process. Now itâs making a series of changes, including layoffs, that may prove painful.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Official suggests Polish president check social media security after odd tweet from private account
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Poland's minister of digital affairs suggested Friday that President Andrzej Duda check the security of access to his social accounts after a bizarre tweet went out that was almost immediately removed.
item.dc:creator: AP, KRON4

item.title: 13-year-old gamer becomes the first to beat the 'unbeatable' Tetris — by breaking it
item.description: The falling-block video game Tetris has met its match in 13-year-old Willis Gibson, who has become the first player to officially “beat” the original Nintendo version of the game — by breaking it.
item.dc:creator: David Hamilton, WFLA, NBC
